title: Woman with Alzheimer's starts conversing again after taking psilocybin
description: A woman with severe Alzheimer's disease who hadn't spoken more than single syllables in years began initiating conversation after a single dose of psilocybin
author: Mattha Busby

title: New-to-science spider builds trap that flings ants into the air
description: A spider living in the rainforests of Queensland, Australia, builds a snare trap reminiscent of a Roman-era ballista weapon that it uses to catapult green tree ants into a web 30 centimetres above
author: James Woodford

title: A promising natural technique to remove CO2 could backfire
description: Several start-ups have tried to grow seaweed to remove atmospheric CO2, but this could affect the levels of nutrients in the ocean and hamper other CO2-sucking processes
author: Alec Luhn

title: People training new AI models admit they just get chatbots to do it
description: The next generation of AI models are meant to be trained by people paid to have conversations with them, but several of these workers have admitted to New Scientist that they simply get chatbots to do it instead. This "AI inbreeding" may reduce the power and usefulness of future models, warn experts
author: Matthew Sparkes

title: Faecal transplant makes the brains of old mice act young again
description: Older mice that received a faecal microbiome transplant from younger animals went on to have improved brain plasticity, which suggests their brains could overcome a neurological condition that is typically successfully treated only in childhood
author: Chris Simms

title: We've found a mysterious substance on Titan and Pluto
description: Something is absorbing light on the surfaces of Pluto and Saturn’s moon Titan, and figuring out what it is could be crucial to understanding Titan’s complex chemistry
author: Leah Crane
